preamble: The open-mid front unrounded vowel /ɛ/ can undergo a transformation into the voiced palatal approximant /j/, particularly in specific morphophonological contexts. This shift involves the vowel losing its vocalic quality and becoming a glide, often as part of a grammatical inflection. While sometimes described as phonologically unmotivated in a universal sense, it represents a regular conversion within the specific grammatical system where it occurs.
phoneticEffects: Vowel to glide conversion, consonantization, reduction of vocalic prominence, palatalization

examples.note: In French verbal inflection, the underlying /ɛ/ of certain suffixes (specifically the 1st and 2nd plural Imperfect Indicative, Present Conditional, and Present Subjunctive) is converted to /j/.

sources: El Fenne, F.-Z. (2020). Paradigm Structure in French Verbal Inflection. Scripta, 24(51), 103-135.
